https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/infrastru.title: Infrastructure work completed in preparation of geothermal drilling in Mataloko, Indonesia
https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/infrastru.plain_summary: Infrastructure work has been finished at the Mataloko geothermal project in Indonesia, preparing the site for the drilling phase—the process of creating holes in the ground to access hot rock deep below the surface. PT PLN, an Indonesian energy company, is leading this project to harness geothermal energy.

https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/omv-and-e.title: OMV and Energie Steiermark announce joint geothermal project in Graz, Austria
https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/omv-and-e.plain_summary: Two companies, OMV and Energie Steiermark, have partnered to develop a geothermal heating project in Graz, Austria. Geothermal heating uses hot rock underground to provide warmth to buildings. They plan to start exploration drilling—the process of making holes deep into the earth to find and assess hot rock resources—by the end of 2026.

https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.title: Fervo Energy Adapts Oil Drilling Techniques to Develop Universal Geothermal Energy Solution - CPG Click Oil and Gas
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.plain_summary: Fervo Energy is using techniques borrowed from the oil and gas industry to develop geothermal energy from superhot rock deep underground. By adapting proven drilling methods, the company aims to create a geothermal solution that can work almost anywhere, making it easier to tap into Earth's heat as a renewable energy source.

https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/carrying-.title: Carrying on the legacy of geothermal innovation at the Utah FORGE project
https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/carrying-.plain_summary: The Utah FORGE project continues to advance Enhanced Geothermal Systems (EGS), which are engineered reservoirs created by injecting water into hot rock deep underground to generate electricity. The project has already made significant contributions to developing this technology and plans to continue its research efforts.

https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/husum-ger.title: Husum, Germany to undertake feasibility study for geothermal heating project
https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/husum-ger.plain_summary: A utility company in Husum, Germany has funded a feasibility study to explore whether geothermal heating—using heat from beneath the Earth's surface—could work for their region. The study will examine the technical and economic viability of tapping into underground heat sources to provide heating to the community.

https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/preparato.title: Preparatory work on Geothermal Frontier Project sites in Japan to start this year
https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/preparato.plain_summary: Japan's government agency JOGMEC (Japan Oil, Gas and Metals National Corporation) is beginning preliminary work at three new geothermal energy sites as part of the Geothermal Frontier Project. This preparatory phase involves surveying and preparing these locations before full development can begin.

https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/bgs-relea.title: BGS releases second version of the UK geothermal catalogue
https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/bgs-relea.plain_summary: The British Geological Survey has released an updated UK geothermal catalogue with nearly 14,000 new data points. This catalogue is a collection of information about underground rock and heat conditions across the UK that helps identify where geothermal energy projects could be developed.

https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/call-for-.title: Call for abstracts – 52nd Stanford Geothermal Workshop, 8-10 February 2027
https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/call-for-.plain_summary: The Stanford Geothermal Workshop, a major annual conference on geothermal energy technology and research, is now accepting abstracts for its 52nd edition scheduled for February 8-10, 2027. Researchers and professionals can submit their work for presentation, with the option to participate remotely.

https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/deep-clos.title: Deep closed-loop geothermal well drilled in Henan Province, China
https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/deep-clos.plain_summary: Wanjiang New Energy has built a deep closed-loop geothermal system in Henan Province, China, which uses pipes that circulate fluid down into hot rock deep underground and back up to capture heat for building warmth. This type of system taps into Earth's natural heat without requiring water extraction, making it a sustainable heating solution.

https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.title: Enhanced Geothermal Systems Demonstration Projects - Department of Energy (.gov)
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.plain_summary: The Department of Energy is funding demonstration projects for Enhanced Geothermal Systems (EGS), which are engineered reservoirs created by fracturing hot rock deep underground and circulating fluid through it to extract heat for energy. These projects are testing whether EGS technology can reliably produce electricity from superhot rock formations where natural geothermal conditions don't exist.

https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/webinar-p.title: Webinar – Prospecting methods to reveal geothermal potential of Eastern Malaysia, 7 July 2026
https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/webinar-p.plain_summary: A webinar on July 7, 2026 will teach people how to use geothermal data (measurements of heat from the Earth) and magnetic data (information about magnetic fields underground) to find places in Eastern Malaysia where geothermal energy could be developed.

https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/herrenkne.title: Herrenknecht, Badenova plan geothermal JV in Germany
https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/herrenkne.plain_summary: Two companies, Herrenknecht Vertical and Badenova, are partnering to develop geothermal heating projects in southwest Germany, beginning with a project called Hartheim. Geothermal heating uses heat from beneath the Earth's surface to warm buildings and communities.

https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/super-ear.title: Super early-bird tickets now available for GeoTHERM 2027 in Offenburg, Germany
https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/super-ear.plain_summary: GeoTHERM, a major geothermal industry conference and exhibition, is opening early registration for its 2027 event in Offenburg, Germany at discounted rates. This event brings together geothermal professionals to discuss technology, projects, and industry developments.

https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/germering.title: Germering-Puchheim, Germany geothermal project progresses with federal funding
https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/germering.plain_summary: A geothermal heating project involving three German cities—Germering, Puchheim, and Munich—has received government funding to move forward. The project will now enter a feasibility study phase, which is an assessment to determine if the project is technically and economically viable before construction begins.

https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/geotherma.title: Geothermal in focus at EU tripartite agreement on energy storage
https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/geotherma.plain_summary: The European Union has signed a new agreement to speed up energy storage solutions across Europe, with geothermal energy included as part of the plan. This agreement brings together multiple parties to support how geothermal systems can store and provide energy more effectively.

https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/pertamina.title: Pertamina celebrates 100 years of geothermal exploration in Indonesia
https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/pertamina.plain_summary: Indonesia's state energy company Pertamina is marking 100 years since geothermal exploration began at Kamojang, a site that harnesses heat from deep underground rock to generate electricity and support local industries. This milestone shows how long-term geothermal development can provide reliable energy and economic benefits to a region.

https://www.canarymedia.com/articles/ele.title: How an NYC high-rise is keeping cool with geothermal energy
https://www.canarymedia.com/articles/ele.plain_summary: A building in Manhattan called 555 Greenwich Street is using an innovative cooling system based on geothermal energy piles—structures that tap into stable underground temperatures about 120 feet below the surface. These 68 piles help keep the building cool by exchanging heat with the earth, which maintains a more consistent temperature than the air above ground.

https://www.canarymedia.com/articles/geo.title: Quaise Energy raises $134M to fuel superhot geothermal ambitions
https://www.canarymedia.com/articles/geo.plain_summary: Quaise Energy, a startup focused on superhot rock geothermal technology (using extremely hot rock deep underground to generate electricity), has raised $134 million in new funding. This brings their total funding to $230 million, which they'll use to build their first commercial power plant in Oregon that will generate 50 megawatts of electricity—enough to power tens of thousands of homes.

https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.title: Quaise Energy raises $134M to fuel superhot geothermal ambitions - Canary Media
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.plain_summary: Quaise Energy, a company focused on superhot rock geothermal technology (using extreme heat from deep underground rock to generate power), has raised $134 million in funding to advance its projects. This financial boost will help the company develop and commercialize its methods for drilling deeper into the Earth to access hotter geothermal resources.

https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.title: MIT spinout raises $134M to advance three-mile drilling into Earth’s superhot rock - Interesting Engineering
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.plain_summary: An MIT-based company has raised $134 million in funding to develop technology for drilling three miles deep into the Earth to access superhot rock (extremely hot rock formations used to generate geothermal energy). This funding will help advance their drilling capabilities and bring superhot rock geothermal technology closer to commercial viability.

https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.title: Quaise Energy Raises $134M to Advance Superhot Geothermal Power Plant - citybiz
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.plain_summary: Quaise Energy, a company developing superhot rock (SHR) geothermal technology—which taps into extremely hot rock deep underground to generate electricity—has raised $134 million in funding. This investment will help the company advance its plans to build geothermal power plants that can access and extract energy from much deeper in the Earth than traditional geothermal wells.

https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.title: Quaise Energy raises $144 million to drill for superhot geothermal power - Startup Fortune
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.plain_summary: Quaise Energy, a startup focused on superhot rock (SHR) geothermal technology—which taps into extremely hot rock deep underground to generate power—has raised $144 million in funding. This investment will support their efforts to develop drilling techniques capable of reaching the superhot temperatures needed to make geothermal energy more viable as a renewable power source.

https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.title: Quaise Energy Closes $134 Million Series B Financing for Superhot Geothermal Drilling - iGrow News
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.plain_summary: Quaise Energy, a company developing superhot rock geothermal technology (drilling into extremely hot rock deep underground to generate electricity), has raised $134 million in funding to advance its drilling capabilities. This financing will help the company develop the specialized equipment and techniques needed to reach the superhot rock layers that can provide a more efficient and scalable source of geothermal energy.

https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.title: Project Obsidian, world’s first superhot geothermal power plant secures $134M funding - Construction Review
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.plain_summary: Project Obsidian, described as the world's first superhot geothermal power plant, has secured $134 million in funding. Superhot rock geothermal technology taps into extremely hot rock deep underground to generate electricity, and this project represents a major milestone in developing this emerging energy source.

https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.title: Hephae Energy Technology Raises $17.8 Million for Commercial Deployment of Superhot Drilling Tech to Advance - EIN News
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.plain_summary: Hephae Energy Technology has secured $17.8 million in funding to commercialize superhot rock (SHR) drilling technology, which involves drilling deep into extremely hot rock formations beneath the Earth's surface to access geothermal energy. This funding will help move the technology from development into real-world deployment for generating clean energy.

https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.title: Geothermal Startup Raises $134 Million for Drilling Superhot Rocks - Bloomberg.com
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.plain_summary: A geothermal startup has secured $134 million in funding to develop superhot rock (SHR) geothermal technology, which taps into extremely hot rock formations deep underground to generate electricity. This capital injection supports the company's efforts to drill into these deep, hot rock layers and extract geothermal energy as a renewable power source.

https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.title: Geothermal Startup Raises $134 Million for Drilling Superhot Rocks - Energy Connects
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.plain_summary: A geothermal startup has secured $134 million in funding to develop drilling technology for superhot rock (SHR) geothermal energy, which involves accessing extremely hot rock deep underground to generate electricity. This funding will support the company's efforts to drill deeper and hotter than traditional geothermal wells, potentially unlocking geothermal energy in locations where it wasn't previously viable.

https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/from-pote.title: From potential to execution: Looking back at the World Geothermal Congress 2026
https://www.thinkgeoenergy.com/from-pote.plain_summary: The World Geothermal Congress 2026 highlighted a shift in the geothermal industry from discussing possibilities to actually building and deploying projects. The conference featured a wider range of topics and participants, reflecting the industry's focus on turning geothermal energy concepts into working systems.

https://www.energy.gov/hgeo/geothermal/a.title: FORGE: Creating Pathways for Geothermal
https://www.energy.gov/hgeo/geothermal/a.plain_summary: FORGE is an initiative aimed at increasing awareness and adoption of geothermal energy, which harnesses heat from beneath the Earth's surface to generate power. The program focuses on creating opportunities and pathways for geothermal technology development and deployment.

https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.title: Quaise Energy Series B Funding: $134M for Superhot Geothermal and Project Obsidian - News and Statistics - IndexBox
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.plain_summary: Quaise Energy has secured $134 million in Series B funding to advance superhot rock geothermal technology, which aims to tap into extremely hot rock deep underground as an energy source. The company is using this investment to develop Project Obsidian, an initiative focused on practical applications of superhot geothermal energy generation.

https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.title: Quaise Energy raises $134m to advance superhot geothermal power project - TradeArabia
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.plain_summary: Quaise Energy, a company developing superhot rock geothermal technology (which taps into extremely hot rock deep underground to generate power), has secured $134 million in funding to advance its project. This investment will help the company move forward with developing systems to access and extract heat from these deep, superhot rock formations for clean energy generation.

https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.title: An MIT Spinout Just Raised $134 Million to Drill for Superhot Rock in Oregon - Startup Fortune
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.plain_summary: A company created by MIT researchers has secured $134 million in funding to develop superhot rock geothermal technology in Oregon. This technology aims to tap into extremely hot rock deep underground to generate electricity, offering a potential new source of clean energy that doesn't depend on traditional geothermal hotspots.

https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.title: Hephae Energy Technology Raises $17.8 Million Series A To Commercialize Superhot Drilling Technology - Pulse 2.0
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.plain_summary: Hephae Energy Technology has secured $17.8 million in Series A funding to advance superhot rock drilling technology, which involves creating boreholes (holes drilled deep into the earth) that can reach extremely high temperatures for geothermal energy production. This investment will help the company move its specialized drilling techniques from the development stage toward commercial deployment.

https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.title: Hephae Energy Technology Raises $17.8 Million for Commercial Deployment of Superhot Drilling Tech to Advance Geothermal - EIN Presswire
https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBM.plain_summary: Hephae Energy Technology has secured $17.8 million in funding to commercialize its superhot rock drilling technology, which is designed to enable geothermal energy production by accessing extremely hot rock formations deep underground. This funding will support the company's efforts to move from development stage to real-world deployment of their specialized drilling techniques for superhot rock geothermal projects.
